name: NIST SP 800-177 Rev. 1 — Trustworthy Email
description: Email security control scoring derived from NIST SP 800-177r1 recommendations. Weights and rating bands are adjustable here or overridden via config.yaml (scoring:).

very_strong_requirements.description: In addition to the score threshold, 'very_strong' requires all core controls in enforcing mode.

spf.name: SPF
spf.recommendation: Publish an SPF record ending in '-all'; keep DNS-querying mechanisms <= 10.

dkim.name: DKIM
dkim.recommendation: Sign outbound mail with DKIM using RSA >= 2048 bits or Ed25519; remove testing flag.

dmarc.name: DMARC
dmarc.recommendation: Publish DMARC with p=reject (pct=100) and aggregate reporting (rua).

starttls.name: STARTTLS
starttls.recommendation: All MX hosts must offer STARTTLS with TLS >= 1.2.

dnssec.name: DNSSEC
dnssec.recommendation: Sign the zone with DNSSEC; ensure DS records at the parent validate.

dane.name: DANE/TLSA
dane.recommendation: Publish TLSA records for every MX host (requires valid DNSSEC).

mta_sts.name: MTA-STS
mta_sts.recommendation: Publish an MTA-STS policy in enforce mode covering all MX hosts.

tlsrpt.name: TLS-RPT
tlsrpt.recommendation: Publish a TLS-RPT record (_smtp._tls) to receive transport failure reports.

bimi.name: BIMI
bimi.recommendation: Optional: publish a BIMI record with a Verified Mark Certificate. Requires DMARC at enforcement.
